summary: Tests are never optional — the Testing Gate is the 60-second decision that maps a change's nature × stakes to the verification type(s) required.

rules.rule: Before GO, answer the two Gate questions — what does the change touch, and what are the stakes — and if they cannot be answered, the design is not ready.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Select the required verification type(s) from the nature × stakes matrix as the default routing, scaling depth with stakes — the trivial path does not carry the full set.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: No change that alters behaviour merges with zero verification; only non-behavioural changes (typo, comment, log line, rename, formatting, pure move, exempt file classes) require no check.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Never wire mutation testing as a CI hard-gate or per-file requirement — it is an opt-in strength lens; for the top-stakes row (auth/payments/tenant-isolation) it is recommended and, if skipped, the skip is stated in one line in the work-unit's verification field.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Verify AI/model-driven behaviour with an eval scored against a rubric — for governed AI behaviour the rubric is the canon-contract itself; for ungoverned behaviour the ad-hoc rubric is declared in the work-unit.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: When the change is high-complexity or ambiguous, escalate the verification strategy to an independent advisor who runs the architecture review in a fresh context — the one who builds does not grade.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
